personalInfo.name: Karam Matar
personalInfo.title: Software Engineer
personalInfo.bio: Software developer with experience in agile development and full-stack systems. Proficient in C++, Java, and SQL, with a background in low-level software and security-focused DevOps tooling. Currently interning at Softhouse and writing my bachelor thesis on an AI-driven feedback loop in CI/CD pipelines.

paragraphs: Hello! I'm Karam Matar, a software engineer based in Sweden. I am currently completing my B.Sc. in Software Engineering at Blekinge Institute of Technology, following a recent exchange semester at Newcastle University focused on security and AI.

Currently, I am a Software Developer Intern at Softhouse, where I serve as the main point of contact between developers and customers. I am also finishing my bachelor thesis in collaboration with a company, designing and evaluating an AI-driven task-to-code feedback loop that integrates into CI/CD pipelines — using LLM API calls, the Model Context Protocol (MCP), and autonomous agent orchestration to automate bug fixing and pull request generation.

contactContent.sectionDescription: Feel free to reach out! Best way to reach me is through email.
contactContent.ctaDescription: Whether you have a question or just want to say hi, I'll try my best to get back to you as soon as possible!

skillsContent.learningTitle: Always learning and exploring new technologies. Currently interested in:

footerContent.tagline: Software Engineer focused on systems, compilers, and full-stack development

description: Serving as the main point of contact between a team of 11 full-stack developers, the customer, and the Head of Department to coordinate requirements and project updates.
Working in a full-stack environment with rotating roles to develop features, handle technical documentation, and ensure clear communication within the team.
Contributing to requirements analysis, coding, and code reviews as part of an agile development process.

experiences.location: Remote via Blekinge Institute of Technology, Sweden
description: Collaborated with a team of six to build a tool for Trafikverket that automatically checks for vulnerabilities in third-party packages.
Refactored legacy PowerShell script into JavaScript to create an Azure DevOps pipeline task that generates SBOM files in JSON format.
Took initiative in team communication by keeping absent team members informed and actively following up to help resolve blockers during development.
Mentored first year students in C++ through lab supervision and one on one tutoring, focusing on core programming logic and best practices.
Assisted students in debugging and mastering low-level concepts, including pointers, memory management, and object-oriented design.
Guided students in analyzing program behavior and performance to ensure code correctness and efficiency.

details: Focus: systems programming, memory management, data structures, and building efficient software.
Bachelor Thesis: Design and evaluation of an AI-driven task-to-code feedback loop in CI/CD pipelines, developed in collaboration with a company.
Relevant Coursework: Compiler Design, Operating Systems, Algorithms & Data Structures, Linear Algebra.
Focus: data analysis and cleaning datasets, basic machine learning algorithms, secure software development, and threat analysis.
Systems design for games, focusing on prototyping mechanics and visual communication.
Relevant Coursework: Computer Vision & AI, Algorithm Design and Analysis, Security Programming, Game Design.

projects.title: ACID Bot — Bachelor Thesis
projects.description: Multi-agent AI system that fetches tasks from ClickUp via MCP, autonomously generates code fixes using Claude Code inside Docker, and opens pull requests via gh cli — with scheduled runs and a GitHub Actions pipeline option.
projects.longDescription: Designed and evaluated an autonomous AI feedback loop for CI/CD pipelines as a bachelor thesis, developed in collaboration with a company. The system uses an orchestrator script coordinating multiple agents: it connects to ClickUp via the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to fetch task descriptions, generates code changes using Claude (initially via API calls, later migrated to Claude Code running directly in a Docker container), and manages repositories and pull requests through gh cli. The pipeline supports both scheduled automated runs via Docker and on-demand execution through a GitHub Actions workflow.

projects.title: Compiler from Scratch
projects.description: End-to-end compiler including lexer, parser, semantic analysis, CFG-based IR, TAC generation, and a custom virtual machine.
projects.longDescription: Built an end-to-end compiler in C++ implementing lexical analysis, parsing, semantic analysis, control-flow graph based intermediate representation, three-address code generation, and a custom virtual machine for execution.

projects.title: VulnerabilityChecker 2.0
projects.description: Refactored legacy PowerShell script into JavaScript to create an Azure DevOps pipeline task that scans third-party packages for vulnerabilities and generates SBOM files.
projects.longDescription: Designed and implemented an Azure DevOps pipeline task for detecting vulnerabilities in third-party dependencies across multiple repositories, parsing dependency manifests, querying vulnerability data, and generating Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) files in JSON format for CI/CD workflows.

projects.title: GyaGame
projects.description: Developed a 2D semi-open-world shooter game in Java using a provided graphics library, focusing on game logic, entity management, and player interaction.
projects.longDescription: A Java game project featuring core gameplay systems such as movement, shooting, and game logic, built using a provided graphics library.

projects.title: ShowcaseSERL
projects.description: Built a full-stack web application to showcase student and researcher projects. Features include dynamic listings, detailed project views, and a kiosk mode, with a focus on component-based architecture and clean data handling.
projects.longDescription: A Next.js web app for showcasing projects with dynamic listings, detailed views, and kiosk mode support, built with a focus on component-based architecture and clean data handling.

projects.title: Space Invaders
projects.description: Created a Space Invaders game in C++ using the SFML graphics library, implementing core game loops, collision detection, and rendering.
projects.longDescription: A Space Invaders clone built in C++ using SFML, focusing on core game loop, input handling, collision logic, and classic arcade gameplay.

projects.title: Death Marching Souls
projects.description: Developed a 3D action-platformer featuring player progression, enemy AI, boss encounters, and UI systems, implementing health management, experience-based leveling, and modular enemy behavior.
projects.longDescription: A 3D Unity game featuring traversal, progression, and boss combat, built to practice gameplay systems including health management, experience-based leveling, and modular enemy behavior.

seo.title: Karam Matar | Software Engineer
seo.description: Karam Matar is a software engineer focused on full-stack development, AI-driven automation, DevOps tooling, and game development. Currently researching AI feedback loops in CI/CD pipelines using MCP and LLM APIs.
